import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    input()  # empty line
    m, n = map(int, input().split())
    p = []
    for i in range(m):
        row = list(map(int, input().split()))
        p.append(row)
    
    # For each friend j, max joy across shops
    max_joy = [max(p[i][j] for i in range(m)) for j in range(n)]
    
    # Collect all values, binary search
    vals = sorted(set(v for row in p for v in row))
    
    lo, hi = 0, len(vals) - 1
    ans = 0
    while lo <= hi:
        mid = (lo + hi) // 2
        alpha = vals[mid]
        # Check condition 1: every friend coverable
        ok1 = all(max_joy[j] >= alpha for j in range(n))
        # Check condition 2: some shop covers 2+ friends
        ok2 = any(sum(1 for j in range(n) if p[i][j] >= alpha) >= 2 for i in range(m))
        if ok1 and ok2:
            ans = alpha
            lo = mid + 1
        else:
            hi = mid - 1
    print(ans)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

import sys
from bisect import bisect_left

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    idx = 0
    t = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
    
    for _ in range(t):
        m = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        
        p = []
        for i in range(m):
            row = [int(input_data[idx+j]) for j in range(n)]
            idx += n
            p.append(row)
        
        # Collect all unique values for binary search
        all_vals = sorted(set(v for row in p for v in row))
        
        def feasible(alpha):
            # Check all friends have at least one valid shop
            # Check at least one shop covers >= 2 friends
            friend_covered = [False] * n
            has_pair = False
            for row in p:
                cnt = 0
                for j in range(n):
                    if row[j] >= alpha:
                        friend_covered[j] = True
                        cnt += 1
                if cnt >= 2:
                    has_pair = True
            return has_pair and all(friend_covered)
        
        lo, hi = 0, len(all_vals) - 1
        ans = all_vals[0] - 1
        # Actually binary search
        lo, hi = 0, len(all_vals) - 1
        ans = -1
        while lo <= hi:
            mid = (lo + hi) // 2
            if feasible(all_vals[mid]):
                ans = all_vals[mid]
                lo = mid + 1
            else:
                hi = mid - 1
        
        print(ans)

solve()

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    input()  # empty line
    m, n = map(int, input().split())
    p = []
    for i in range(m):
        row = list(map(int, input().split()))
        p.append(row)
    
    # For each friend j, max joy across shops
    max_joy = [max(p[i][j] for i in range(m)) for j in range(n)]
    
    # Collect all values, binary search
    vals = sorted(set(v for row in p for v in row))
    
    lo, hi = 0, len(vals) - 1
    ans = 0
    while lo <= hi:
        mid = (lo + hi) // 2
        alpha = vals[mid]
        # Check condition 1: every friend coverable
        ok1 = all(max_joy[j] >= alpha for j in range(n))
        # Check condition 2: some shop covers 2+ friends
        ok2 = any(sum(1 for j in range(n) if p[i][j] >= alpha) >= 2 for i in range(m))
        if ok1 and ok2:
            ans = alpha
            lo = mid + 1
        else:
            hi = mid - 1
    print(ans)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

import sys
from bisect import bisect_left

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    idx = 0
    t = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
    
    for _ in range(t):
        m = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        
        p = []
        for i in range(m):
            row = [int(input_data[idx+j]) for j in range(n)]
            idx += n
            p.append(row)
        
        # Collect all unique values for binary search
        all_vals = sorted(set(v for row in p for v in row))
        
        def feasible(alpha):
            # Check all friends have at least one valid shop
            # Check at least one shop covers >= 2 friends
            friend_covered = [False] * n
            has_pair = False
            for row in p:
                cnt = 0
                for j in range(n):
                    if row[j] >= alpha:
                        friend_covered[j] = True
                        cnt += 1
                if cnt >= 2:
                    has_pair = True
            return has_pair and all(friend_covered)
        
        lo, hi = 0, len(all_vals) - 1
        ans = all_vals[0] - 1
        # Actually binary search
        lo, hi = 0, len(all_vals) - 1
        ans = -1
        while lo <= hi:
            mid = (lo + hi) // 2
            if feasible(all_vals[mid]):
                ans = all_vals[mid]
                lo = mid + 1
            else:
                hi = mid - 1
        
        print(ans)

solve()
